text.title: Refusal Field Strict Schema Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every refusal-field-strict-schema spec MUST enforce.

rule.statement: The output schema MUST include a top-level optional string `refusal` field.
rule.rationale: Top-level makes branching cheap; nested means every consumer has to drill in.

rule.statement: When using OpenAI Responses API, `strict: true` MUST be set on the schema.
rule.rationale: Non-strict mode lets the model wander outside the schema.

rule.statement: Caller code MUST check `output.refusal` before processing other fields; on non-null, return early.
rule.rationale: Without branching, the agent acts on stub/empty fields and corrupts state.

rule.statement: Refusal text MUST be logged for safety review.
rule.rationale: Refusal patterns inform prompt or policy adjustments.

rule.statement: Prompts MUST NOT instruct the model to refuse; the model picks based on policy, the schema accommodates.
